title: execution-spec-v1
description: Input contract, platform -> executor. Persisted by the control plane and handed to the Go execution engine. Carries references and non-secret data only: no host, no path, no argv, no environment variable, no password, no token, no key, no passphrase, no credential reference, and no output_dir (the workspace belongs to the worker, not to the persisted spec). Version 1 governs a dry run and nothing else. Unknown fields are rejected at every level: a field the executor does not understand may be a field the operator believes is being honoured. This schema describes structure; cross-field rules (scope must select at least one area, mailbox_filter requires mail, domain_filter requires mail or files) live in the semantic validators internal/executioncontract/contract.go and domain/execution_contract.py.

format_version.description: Document format version. Absent, zero, and future versions are rejected; there is no silent downgrade. Not the executor build version, which appears only as execution-result-v1.version.

run_id.description: Run identifier. Same rules as events.ValidateRunID: non-empty, at most 128 characters, no forward slash, no backslash, no NUL byte. The NUL exclusion is enforced by the semantic validators; it is not expressible as a JSON Schema pattern.

plan_id.description: The migration plan this execution is anchored to: the plan the operator actually saw.

source_snapshot_id.description: Inventory snapshot of the source, as it was when the plan was produced.

destination_snapshot_id.description: Inventory snapshot of the destination, as it was when the plan was produced.

comparison_report_id.description: Comparison report the plan derives its severities from.

mode.description: Only dry_run in v1. Note the underscore: the executor's report emits mode as dry-run (hyphen), apply, or account-inventory. Input and output modes are different enums over different vocabularies and must never be merged.

scope.title: spec scope
scope.description: What the run covers. The three booleans are required and never defaulted: an absent mail must not mean false in one language and an error in the other. DNS is absent by design — it stays inventoried, compared and surfaced as a manual task, never auto-run.

domain_filter.description: Restrict the run to one domain. Valid only when mail or files is true.

mailbox_filter.description: Restrict the run to one mailbox. Valid only when mail is true.
